question: Extend the `Vehicle` hierarchy. Add a virtual `displayInfo()` method to the base `Vehicle` class that prints general vehicle info. Override this method in `Car` and `Motorcycle` to include their specific attributes, demonstrating runtime polymorphism.

test_cases.expected:
Brand: Toyota, Year: 2020, Doors: 4
Brand: Harley-Davidson, Year: 2022, Sidecar: No
Brand: Honda, Year: 2018, Doors: 2

errors: The `displayInfo()` method in the base `Vehicle` class is not declared `virtual`. This prevents runtime polymorphism, causing static dispatch where `Vehicle::displayInfo()` is always called through base pointers, instead of the derived class overrides, despite the derived classes having their own `displayInfo()` methods.
